ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 301, HEARING HELD. NOT CONTESTED, NOT ARGUED, AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF IS GRANTED UNDER C.C.P. SECTION 36(A). OPPOSITION WITHDRAWN. THE TRIAL DATE IS JULY 8, 2024 AT 11:15 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 206. LAST DAY PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 36(F) IS JULY 16, 2024. A FURTHER CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE IS SET FOR MAY 21, 2024 AT 1:30 P.M. A MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE IS SET FOR THURSDAY, JUNE 27, 2024, AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 503. JUDGE: ROCHELLE C. EAST. CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA. DEPUTY: NOT PRESENT. MATTER NOT REPORTED. (301/RCE) ** ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE WAS SIGNED AND FILED ON 3/20/24. **
ASBESTOS SETTLEMENT CALENDAR OF JUN-27-2024 IS OFF CALENDAR - NOT HELD AND SETTLED. JUDGE: SAMUEL K. FENG, CLERK: A. MOK. NOT REPORTED. (503)

PLAINTIFF REX HANCOCK MOTION FOR PREFERENCE / PREFERENTIAL SETTING (ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR TENTATIVE RULING ENTRY PURPOSES ONLY.) (301)
Off Calendar Mar-19-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
(Part 2 of 2)
(Mini minutes continued from previous entry)

9. A further case management conference is set for May 21, 2024 at 1:30 p.m.
a. Case management conference statements are not required.
b. Parties may appear at the conference by zoom.
c. If the parties agree that no CMC is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two court days before the hearing.

10. Plaintiffs will send a settlement demand to Defendants no later than May 28, 2024. Response to the settlement demand is due June 10, 2024. The parties shall separately submit confidential settlement demands/offers. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Department503@sftc.org via electronic mail on June 17, 2024.

11. A mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, June 27, 2024, at 9:30 a.m. in Department 503. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is excused for good cause. A request to excuse a personal appearance must be submitted to Department503@sftc.org two weeks before the mandatory settlement conference.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Not Present. Matter not reported. (301/RCE)

**After the hearing, the order granting preference signed and filed on 3/20/24.**

MOTION FOR PREFERENCE / PREFERENTIAL SETTING
Off Calendar Mar-19-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via CourtCall:
Thomas A. Steig of PRINDLE, GOETZ, BARNES & REINHOLTZ, LLP (415-788-8354), appearing for Intervenor Hartford Accident and Indemnity Company, et al;
Mary Grace Braun of DLA PIPER (US), LLP (916-699-2700), appearing for defendant 3M Company.

Not contested, not argued, and tentative ruling adopted as follows: Plaintiffs' Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. section 36(a). Opposition withdrawn. Conditional non-opposition filed.

1. The trial date is July 8, 2024 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 206.
a. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is July 16, 2024.
b. The parties shall follow the California Rules of Court, San Francisco Local Rules, and Local Rule 20.

2. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is July 2, 2024.
a. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise.
b. Before a party files and serves a summary judgment/adjudication motion, it must contact the clerk to make a reservation.
c. The Court allows a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared, unless good cause is found to exceed this number. Contact the clerk to schedule a good cause hearing.

3. Time to respond to written discovery not yet served is shortened to 20 days.
a. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier.
b. Any issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, shall occur in person or via telephone, not by email or letter.

4. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service.
5. The fact discovery cut-off date is June 21, 2024.
6. The expert discovery cut-off date is July 5, 2024.

7. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than April 19, 2024.
a. If Plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify Defendants no later than five days after submission.
b. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.

8. No later than April 2, 2024:
a. Plaintiff must provide a statement to each Defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting Plaintiff's claims as to each Defendant.
b. Defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of facts supporting Defendant's affirmative defenses.
c. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary judgment motion. (mini minutes continue in Part 2 of 2)
